_meta.description: Rules for resolving which document version governs based on effective dates, execution status, and version hierarchy.

rules.trigger: Two versions exist: one with a later effective date and status=effective
rules.description: When both documents are effective, the one with the later effective date governs.

labels.pt: Data de vigência mais recente prevalece

rules.trigger: One document is signed/executed, the other is unsigned/draft
rules.description: A signed/executed document always takes precedence over an unsigned/draft version, regardless of date.
'Latest' always refers to the most recent effective date, never the most recent upload/ingestion date.

rules.trigger: Draft version and executed version both exist
rules.description: A draft version never governs over an executed version. Drafts are informational only.

rules.trigger: A restated version exists alongside prior amendments
rules.description: An 'Amended and Restated' document supersedes the original and all prior amendments. Only the restated version governs.

rules.trigger: Extension document exists for an expired agreement
rules.description: An extension document revives an expired agreement with new effective/expiry dates. The extended version governs.

rules.trigger: Termination document exists for an otherwise effective agreement
rules.description: A termination notice or agreement terminates the parent document regardless of its effective date.
